{"tool":"Explore","description":"Scaffold project structure","prompt":"Thoroughly explore the project structure at /home/user/workspace. I need to understand:
1. The top-level directory layout (package.json, tsconfig.json, next.config, etc.)
2. Everything under `pages/` — every file, subdirectory, and their contents
3. Everything under `components/` if it exists
4. Everything under `lib/` or `utils/` if they exist
5. Everything under `public/` if it exists
6. Any `styles/` or CSS files
7. Any API routes under `pages/api/`
8. The layout/header/footer components
9. Any custom App, Document, or other _app/_document files
10. The tsconfig.json and package.json contents

Be very thorough — read every file in the pages directory and all shared components.","subagent_type":"Explore"}
attributes.io.output:
[{"type":"text","text":"Here is a thorough report of the entire project structure.

---

## Project Overview

This is a **Next.js 16 (canary)** blog application built with **React 19** and **TypeScript**, though most page files use `.js` extensions (plain JavaScript with JSX). It fetches data from the JSONPlaceholder API for demo purposes. The project uses `type: "module"` in package.json.

---

## 1. Top-Level Layout

**`/home/user/workspace/package.json`**
- `"private": true`, `"type": "module"`
- Scripts: `dev`, `build`, `start` (standard Next.js)
- Dependencies: `next@16.2.0-canary.41`, `react@19.1.0`, `react-dom@19.1.0`, `ai@5.0.59`, `@ai-sdk/react@2.0.0-beta.28`, `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@^1.17.0`, `zod@^4.0.10`
- Dev dependencies: TypeScript, `@types/node`, `@types/react`, `@types/react-dom`, `vitest`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `vite-tsconfig-paths`

**`/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json`**
- Target: `ES2017`, JSX: `react-jsx`, module: `esnext`, moduleResolution: `bundler`
- Strict mode enabled
- Path alias: `@/*` maps to `./*`
- Includes: `next-env.d.ts`, `**/*.ts`, `**/*.tsx`, `.next/types/**/*.ts`

**`/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts`**
- Empty configuration (default `NextConfig` object with no custom settings)

**`/home/user/workspace/.gitignore`**
- Standard Next.js ignores: `node_modules`, `.next/`, `out/`, `build`, `.env*`, `.vercel`, `coverage`

**No other config files exist** (no `.eslintrc`, `.prettierrc`, `.env` files).

---

## 2. Pages Directory (`pages/`)

All pages use **plain `.js` (JavaScript + JSX)**, not `.tsx`. Here is every file and its role:

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/index.js` -- Home Page
- **Route:** `/`
- **Data fetching:** `getServerSideProps` -- fetches 5 posts from `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts?_limit=5`, also captures `user-agent` from request headers and a `timestamp`.
- **UI:** Renders a welcome heading, the server timestamp, the user agent string, a list of 5 post titles as links (`<a href="/blog/{id}">`), and a "View All Posts" `<button>` that calls `router.push('/blog')`.
- Uses `next/head` for `<title>` and OG meta tags.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/_app.js` -- Custom App
- Imports `AppProvider` (shared context wrapper) and `globals.css`.
- Renders: `<AppProvider>` wrapping a div with class `app-container` that contains:
  - **Header** with site title "My Blog" and a `<nav>` with two `<Link>`s: Home (`/`) and Blog (`/blog`).
  - **Main** area rendering the current page via `<Component {...pageProps} />`.
  - **Footer** with copyright "2024 My Blog".

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/_document.js` -- Custom Document
- Sets `<Html lang="en">`.
- In `<Head>`: meta description, `/favicon.ico` favicon link, Google Fonts preconnect and Inter font stylesheet (weights 400, 500, 600).
- Body contains `<Main />` and `<NextScript />`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/_error.js` -- Custom Error Page
- Displays a status-code-dependent error message ("A {code} error occurred on server" vs "An error occurred on client").
- Handles 404 separately with "This page could not be found."
- Uses `getInitialProps` to extract `statusCode` from `res` or `err` (falls back to 404).
- Uses `next/head` for title.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/404.js` -- Custom 404 Page
- Displays "404 - Page Not Found".
- Provides a `<button>` that calls `router.push('/')` to go home.
- Uses `next/head` for title and description meta.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/index.js` -- Blog List Page
- **Route:** `/blog`
- **Data fetching:** `getStaticProps` with ISR (`revalidate: 60`). Fetches ALL posts from JSONPlaceholder.
- **UI:** "All Blog Posts" heading, then a grid of `.post-card` divs. Each card shows the post title as a link (`/blog/{id}`), a 100-character excerpt of the body, and a "Read More" button using `router.push`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/[id].js` -- Single Blog Post
- **…(+5022)
